title: Reserved Domains Best Source Decision
description: Decide which public source is the best technical starting point for explaining reserved, example and special-use DNS names.
question: Which public source is the best technical starting point for explaining reserved, example and special-use DNS names to an operator who needs both normative grounding and practical coverage?

sources.label: iana_reserved
sources.notes: Institutional overview of reserved and policy-special domains.

sources.label: rfc_2606
sources.notes: Best Current Practice defining reserved example names.

decision_criteria.label: Normative Authority
decision_criteria.description: The source should look like a primary normative or standards-oriented reference, not just a short overview.
decision_criteria.notes: Favor standards-facing sources over short institutional summaries.

decision_criteria.label: Example Name Coverage
decision_criteria.description: The source should explicitly cover the reserved example names and the localhost/invalid family.
decision_criteria.notes: Coverage matters more than prose style here.

decision_criteria.label: Special-Use Scope
decision_criteria.description: The source should make special-use domain behavior and scope explicit, not only list examples.
decision_criteria.notes: This criterion is intentionally weighted high because the question asks for a technical starting point.

decision_criteria.label: Operator Overview
decision_criteria.description: The source should still help an operator quickly understand practical registry or documentation context.
decision_criteria.notes: This keeps the decision from overfitting to purely normative text.
